titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jane B. Hendrick papers relating to James G. Barnett
Hendrick, Jane B. papers relating to James G. Barnett
titlestmt.author: Karen Spicher

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 03:36

did.unittitle: Jane B. Hendrick papers relating to James G. Barnett

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English; some vocal music texts in Latin.

did.abstract: The Jane B. Hendrick papers relating to James G. Barnett contain sacred vocal music and other vocal music by Barnett, 1859-1886 and undated, including autograph manuscript scores, scores and parts copied by Hendrick and unidentified copyists, and published music. Some music is accompanied by autograph manuscript notes by Hendrick about performance, editing, and publication, or by printed performance programs. Also present are additional autograph manuscript notes by Hendrick about Barnett's music, undated; music notebooks written by Hendrick while a student of Barnett, 1873 and undated; concert programs and newspaper clippings about performances of music by Barnett, 1860-circa 1885; and an autograph letter signed, from Hendrick to Eva Judd O'Meara, Yale University music librarian, presenting manuscript music by Barnett, 1923 June 13.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings and Related Papers, 1860-1923. II. Music by James G. Barnett, 1859-1886. III. Music by Others, undated.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James G. Barnett (died 1885) (Yale 1867 PhD Hon) composer, conductor, and organist, emigrated from England to Boston, Massachusetts, circa 1845. Barnett was organist at Center Church, Hartford, Connecticut, circa 1845-1867, and conductor of the Beethoven Society, Hartford, Connecticut, 1857-1874. After moving to New Haven, Connecticut, circa 1875, Barnett was organist at St. Thomas Episcopal Church, New Haven, and later at Second Congregational Church, Fair Haven, Connecticut.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jane B. Hendrick studied music with James G. Barnett in the 1870s and later edited his music for publication.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Jane B. Hendrick, circa 1923.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jane B. Hendrick Papers Relating to James G. Barnett. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Jane B. Hendrick papers relating to James G. Barnett contain sacred vocal music and other vocal music by Barnett, 1859-1886 and undated, including autograph manuscript scores, scores and parts copied by Hendrick and unidentified copyists, and published music. Some music is accompanied by autograph manuscript notes by Hendrick about performance, editing, and publication, or by printed performance programs. Also present are additional autograph manuscript notes by Hendrick about Barnett's music, undated; music notebooks written by Hendrick while a student of Barnett, 1873 and undated; concert programs and newspaper clippings about performances of music by Barnett, 1860-circa 1885; and an autograph letter signed, from Hendrick to Eva Judd O'Meara, Yale University music librarian, presenting manuscript music by Barnett, 1923 June 13.

did.unittitle: Writings and related papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains autograph manuscript notes and correspondence relating to Barnett's music; music notebooks written by Hendrick while a student of Barnett; and printed ephemera relating to performance of Barnett's music.

did.unittitle: Notes on music by James G. Barnett
Concert programs and clippings
Music notebooks
Autograph letter, signed, from Jane B. Hendrick to Eva Judd O'Meara
Music exercises and notes
Music by James G. Barnett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music by Barnett is written in autograph manuscript, unless otherwise indicated. Works are for chorus or solo voices with keyboard accompaniment. Parts may be incomplete.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Anniversary ode . Score (incomplete)
Benedictus . In F major. Score; and score (copyist's manuscript)
Benedictus . In B-flat major. Score (incomplete) and parts
Benedictus . In A-flat major. Score
Benedic anima . Parts; and score (copyist's manuscript)
Bonum est . Score and parts; score (copyist's manuscript); and printed score (Philadelphia: North, copyright 1886)
Christ our passover . Sketches and parts; and printed score, 2 copies (Boston: Ditson, copyright 1879)
The Christian's supplication: a sacred cantata . Printed score (Cincinnati: Church, copyright 1886)
Deus misereatur . Score (incomplete); score (copyist's manuscript); and printed score (Philadelphia: North, copyright 1886)
Epiphany hymn . Score
Festival te deum . Score and part; two scores and part (copyist's manuscript)
Gloria in excelsis . In G major. Score and parts
Gloria in excelsis . In G major. Score
Gloria in excelsis . In F major. Printed score (Philadelphia: North, copyright 1886)
Gloria in excelsis . In B-flat major. Score and parts; and score (copyist's manuscript)
Gloria in excelsis . In B-flat major. Score and parts
Hebrew service . Text only (copyist's manuscript)
Hymn to the evening . Score (copyist's manuscript), with notes by Jane B. Hendrick; and printed program (1873 March 16)
Jubilate deo . In B-flat major. Score
Jubilate deo . In E-flat major. Score (incomplete) and parts
Magnificat . Score and parts; and score (copyist's manuscript)
Ode: the master and the craft . Score (mimeograph), 91 copies

odd.head: General
odd.p: First line: Hand in hand, hand in hand, through our temples move.

did.unittitle: Ode: the master and the craft . Parts
Ode to education . Sketches, score, and parts; and printed text (no publisher or date)
Te deum . In B-flat major. Sketches and parts
Venite exultemus domino . Sketches and parts; score (copyist's manuscript); and printed score (Philadelphia: North, copyright 1886)
Unidentified. Sketches
Music by Others
Audran, Edmond (1842-1901). Les noces d'Olivette. I love my love so well (duet). English. Vocal score (copyist's manuscript)
Balfe, M. W. (Michael William) (1808-1870). List, dearest, list (duet). Vocal score (printed fragment, with revisions, in copyist's manuscript)
Verdi, Giuseppe (1813-1901). Attila. Te sol quest'anima . English. Arranged by James G. Barnett. Vocal score (copyist's manuscript)
